activities_projects.title: Literary Theme Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyse deeper themes such as patience, growth, and the human–nature relationship
steps: Read the story carefully and identify major themes
Explain how the cherry tree symbolises patience and perseverance
Provide textual evidence supporting each theme
Present your analysis in class discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop literary interpretation and analytical thinking
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of interpretation and textual evidence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Symbolism in the Story
activities_projects.objective: Understand the symbolic meaning of the cherry tree
steps: Identify elements in the story that may represent symbolic meanings
Discuss what the cherry tree represents in Rakesh’s life
Write a short analytical paragraph explaining the symbolism
Share your explanation with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand symbolism and deeper literary meaning
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of interpretation and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Character Relationship Study
activities_projects.objective: Examine the relationship between Rakesh and his grandfather
steps: Identify important conversations between Rakesh and his grandfather
Explain how the grandfather influences Rakesh’s thinking
Analyse how their relationship develops through the story
Write a short essay on their bond
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand character relationships and influence
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical analysis and textual examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Perspective Writing
activities_projects.objective: Develop creative and imaginative writing skills
steps: Imagine the story from the perspective of the cherry tree
Write a diary entry describing the events experienced by the tree
Include challenges such as animals eating leaves or branches being cut
Share the diary entry in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance creativity and narrative perspective
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and clarity of narration (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Connect the story’s ideas with real-life experiences
steps: Think about a personal experience of caring for a plant or project
Compare your experience with Rakesh’s experience
Identify similarities and differences
Write a reflective paragraph
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect literature with real-life experiences
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reflection and comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Environmental Awareness Debate
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of protecting nature
steps: Discuss how the cherry tree survived many challenges
Form two groups to debate the importance of planting trees
Prepare arguments supporting environmental conservation
Present arguments during the class debate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking and communication skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of arguments and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Narrative Structure Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the structure of the story
steps: Identify the exposition, rising action, climax, and resolution of the story
Write each stage of the narrative structure
Explain how each stage contributes to the overall message
Present your findings to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about narrative structure in literature

activities_projects.title: Figurative Language Identification
activities_projects.objective: Recognise literary devices used in the story
steps: Find examples of descriptive language and imagery in the text
Identify literary devices such as imagery and symbolism
Explain the effect of these devices on the reader
Discuss the examples with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the use of literary techniques
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of identification and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Philosophical Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Interpret the deeper meaning of the story’s ending
steps: Read the final paragraph where Rakesh reflects on the tree
Discuss what Rakesh means by feeling like a creator
Write a reflective explanation of the ending
Share interpretations with classmates
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of interpretation and clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Research Project on Trees
steps: Research different types of fruit trees and their benefits
Collect information about how trees help the environment
Prepare a short presentation or poster
Present your research to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop research and presentation skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and presentation (10 marks)
